steps.title: Spot the key difference
steps.content: The decisive clue is the contrast between wild and lab behavior: daytime is the most active period for wild tucos but not for captive ones. This behavioral difference is what the researchers discovered.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: When organisms behave differently in lab versus wild conditions, the lab setting is interfering with their natural patterns. The other three either contradict the evidence of change, extend conclusions beyond the data, or introduce unsupported comparisons. That matches choice D — D is correct.
